EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A prefrontal trough and a low-level perturbation moved over the islands. With above than normal moisture, showers and thunderstorms developed in the vicinity of the Virgin Islands, streaming toward eastern Puerto Rico. The activity intensified in the vicinity of San Juan, where nearly two inches of rainfall accumulated. Urban street flooding was reported, affecting traffic, though roads remained passable.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Broadcast meteorologist shared on social media a photo of flooding along PR-1, by the port in Old San Juan. The roads were still passable.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A frontal boundary crossed the islands on Christmas Eve, causing significant rainfall activity along portions of the northern and eastern half of Puerto Rico. Periods of heavy showers and thunderstorms resulted in parking spaces and streets flood. Rainfall accumulation ranged from 1 to 3 inches in general, with the highest accumulation reported in San Juan.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Flooding was reported along Avenida Simon Madera near Mall of San Juan. Floodwaters impacted at least one vehicle, with reports of occupants stranded inside.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A long period northerly swell produced large breaking waves and strong rip currents along the northern beaches of the islands late in December. On the 28th, a person drowned after getting caught in a rip current in Loiza, PR.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The police marine unit and emergency managers reported that a 39-year-old man drowned after being caught in a rip current behind Mar-Bella Restaurant in Loiza. A woman was also swept by the rip current at the same location but was successfully rescued by the first responders.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Strong winds associated with a thunderstorm caused multiple trees to fall in the Sector El Tanque, Barrio Arenales Altos. At least one vehicle was damaged by a large tree, and several roads were blocked by debris. No injuries were reported.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A long period northerly swell produced high surf and life-threatening rip currents along the northern coast of the islands during the last weekend of February.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A 62-year-old man drowned while trying to rescue a female with two of his friends in Vacia Talega Beach.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A long-period northerly swell was moving across the local Atlantic waters and Caribbean passages on January 11th, generating large breaking waves along the northerly exposed beaches of Puerto Rico, Vieques, Culebra, and the U.S. Virgin Islands. Breaking wave heights reached around 13 to 15 feet. These conditions resulted in life-threatening rip currents, high surf and coastal erosion. High Surf was reported in western Puerto Rico while Coastal Flood was reported in several areas of northern Puerto Rico.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A professor from the University of Puerto Rico reported sand and vegetation along PR-102 near the Guanajibo River due to large breaking waves in the area.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A fading northerly swell and breezy to locally windy conditions promoted life-threatening rip currents for the northern coast of Puerto Rico, among other areas, on January 19th. A high risk of rip currents was in effect for northern Puerto Rico, due to breaking waves of around 6 feet. A rip current related death occurred in Arecibo before sunrise on January 19th.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A 63-year-old man was fishing with his son at a boat ramp near Playa Abacoa, close to the mouth of Ca��o Tiburones in Arecibo. At approximately 5:40 AM AST, the man lost his footing and fell into the water, where strong rip currents swiftly carried him into open waters. His son immediately alerted emergency services, leading to a rapid mobilization of local resources, including the U.S. Coast Guard. Later that day, his shoe and baseball cap were discovered approximately a mile and a half offshore. The search efforts continued until the afternoon of January 20 (the following day), when his body was found near Club Nautico de Arecibo at around 3 PM.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A landslide was reported in Arecibo at around noon on January 6th but no people were trapped or injured. The nearest station, FW8412 Arecibo, reported 1.20 inches since January 1st, 1.02 inches since January 5th and 0.50 inches since 4:00 AM on January 6th. Radar and satellite analysis indicated a persistent wet pattern that morning, with frequent, fast-moving showers tracking well inland from the local waters, associated with the dissipating remnants of a frontal boundary.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A landslide was reported by Emergency Managers in the interior side of Los Millonarios St. (St. 1) in Arecibo. No people were reported as trapped or injured.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Two nearby frontal boundaries ultimately promoted strong showers along the northern coast of Puerto Rico (among other areas), with rainfall accumulations ranging from 2 to 4 inches from Vega Alta to Quebradillas, and 1 to 2 from Rio Grande to Toa Alta. Some flooded roads was observed in Hatillo and Quebradillas.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Road 130 closed due to flooding near Lechuga Bakery, Lechuga Pharmacy, and Texaco Gas Station 518. Radar estimates indicated around 2 inches close to 3 inches.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A series of large northerly swells impacted the Atlantic coastline of the islands during mid-March. These swells produced large breaking waves, life-threatening rip currents, and minor coastal flooding.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A 61-year-old male tourist from California drowned at Sandy Beach in Barrio Puntas, Rincon, after struggling in the water.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A long-period northwesterly swell spread across the Atlantic from early Saturday morning through Sunday. Large breaking waves of 8 to 12 feet impacted the coastal areas from Rincon and Aguada through the northern coastline of Puerto Rico to Fajardo, as well|as Culebra and the north U.S. Virgin Islands.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A 71-year-old male tourist drowned after being swept away by rip currents at a beach near a hotel on PR-115 in Barrio Caguabo, Anasco.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: From April 26 through 30, a moist and unstable weather pattern affected Puerto Rico and the U.S. Virgin Islands. The episode began as deep tropical moisture moved into the region from the southeast, interacting with deep-layer troughs and supportive jet dynamics aloft. This synoptic setup led to daily rounds of scattered to numerous showers and isolated thunderstorms, especially during the afternoons across the interior and western portions of Puerto Rico.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The 911 call center reported that urban floodwaters have stranded 15 people inside the National Lumber and Hardware Store on Antonio J. Fas Alzamora Road (PR-100) in Cabo Rojo.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Tropical moisture associated with a tropical wave and other trade wind perturbations, along with an upper-level trough resulted in the formation of strong showers and thunderstorms. These showers resulted in flooding, landslide and wind damage along portions of eastern, northern and western Puerto Rico.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A 24-year-old male was swept away from the waterfall pool known as Infinity Pool in El  Yunque by a surge of water, fell from a height of 80 feet and died. He was with three other people who are safe.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Breezy to windy conditions were present to start the month due to tightening of the  pressure gradient over the northern Caribbean as a surface high-pressure |system strengthened over the western central Atlantic.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A fallen tree, brought down by strong winds, knocked over a lamp post on PR-777 at around noon. This occured near Colmado Los Rivera and MMM Aguas Buenas Church in Barrio Caguitas, Aguas Buenas. Additionally, the report indicates that soil in the area was saturated due to recent rainfall.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Enhanced trade winds brought an increase in tropical moisture. Additionally, instability aloft increased due to the vicinity of an upper-level low. These features fuel strong showers and thunderstorms, causing flooding.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A tropical wave, alongside diurnal heating and local effects, promoted showers and thunderstorms over south-central to southwestern Puerto Rico. Ponce, in particular, was heavily affected as Flash Flooding was reported. Between 2 and 4 inches, locally higher, fell during the afternoon to around 6 PM.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Flash flooding was reported in the Ponce Bypass area (main street) near Highway PR 2, with radar estimates indicating approximately 1 to 3 inches of rainfall.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A deep tropical moisture surge contributed to the generation of showers and thunderstorms. As rain accumulated, soil became saturated, and flooding and landslides were registered. Also, wind and lightning damage was also observed within the thunderstorms.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Social media reported flooding and impassable roads at Avenida Luis Vigoreaux and Calle Sirio as Quebrada Margarita reached minor flood stage.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A tropical wave moving over the region on July 27th triggered frequent bands of passing showers over the Anegada Passage, Caribbean, and Atlantic waters during the early morning hours. As the day progressed, scattered showers and thunderstorms developed over the eastern half of Puerto Rico. Some of these thunderstorms impacted northeastern Puerto Rico and produced wind gusts of up to 40 mph and frequent cloud to ground lightning. 3 people were injured by a lightning strike in Fajardo while the side of a roof was damaged by a lightning strike in San Juan.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The 911 Call Center and Emergency Managers reported that 2 female minors and 1 female adult were injured by a lightning strike at the  Balneario Seven Seas, Fajardo, near PR-987 (Cabezas St.). The female minors were reported as stable, conscious and breathing at the time of the report. One of the minors age was not reported but the other one was reported as being 16 years old. The female adult was 21 years old.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A trade wind perturbation resulted in an increased frequency of showers moving over eastern portions of Puerto Rico on July 2nd. Debris Flow was reported later in the afternoon at PR-183, close to the Pedro Rivera Molina Elementary School.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Debris Flow was reported in PR-183 (Jesus Sta. Aponte St.) close to the Pedro Rivera Molina Elementary School. No vehicles or people were reported as trapped.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A tropical wave moving over the region on July 27th triggered frequent bands of passing showers over the Anegada Passage, Caribbean, and Atlantic waters during the early morning hours. As the day progressed, scattered showers and thunderstorms developed over the eastern half of Puerto Rico. Some of these thunderstorms impacted northeastern Puerto Rico and produced wind gusts of up to 40 mph and frequent cloud to ground lightning. 3 people were injured by a lightning strike in Fajardo while the side of a roof was damaged by a lightning strike in San Juan.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Social media post reported a damaged building roof edge at the Condado Real  Condominium in Kings Court Street & McLeary Avenue.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: The flooding threat increased as a robust tropical wave moved across the region. This wave combined with instability associated with an upper-level trough. As the wave departed, tropical moisture continued to flux into Puerto Rico. Showers and thunderstorms were numerous, causing flooding, landslide and wind damage.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A large tree was uprooted and fell, impacting electrical lines. This caused a power outage in a residential area near Centro Medico.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: A member of the public posted an image on social media of a fallen power pole struck by  lightning during thunderstorm activity in Bo. Saltos, Sector La Charca in Orocovis.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Heavy shower and thunderstorm activity was observed each afternoon along the interior and western Puerto Rico. These rains saturated soils, causing a few landslides by the middle of the month.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A social media post showed a landslide along PR-123 at km 28.5 in Adjuntas that sent rocks and debris onto the road, causing a partial closure. The event was attributed to heavy showers in recent days in the mountainous region.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Tropical moisture associated with a tropical wave and other trade wind perturbations, along with an upper-level trough resulted in the formation of strong showers and thunderstorms. These showers resulted in flooding, landslide and wind damage along portions of eastern, northern and western Puerto Rico.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Emergency managers reported two landscape workers swept away by the current in PR-2 (Jose Joaquin Yiye Avila St.), right in front of Las Cascadas Waterpark near Villa Alegria Urbanization. One of the workers had to be transported to a hospital. Their two tractors were also swept away. Flooding was also reported by the media and a NWS Employe along PR-2 (Jose Joaquin Yiye Avila St.) toward GT Tune & Lube.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Enhanced trade winds brought an increase in tropical moisture. Additionally, instability aloft increased due to the vicinity of an upper-level low. These features fuel strong showers and thunderstorms, causing flooding.

There were no reported injuries; the individuals were stranded due to flooding conditions. All four individuals were rescued safely. A flash flood warning was in effect at the time of the incident, with an estimated 2 to 3 inches of rainfall in the area.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Enhanced trade winds brought an increase in tropical moisture. Additionally, instability aloft increased due to the vicinity of an upper-level low. These features fuel strong showers and thunderstorms, causing flooding.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Lingering moisture and marginal instability associated with a nearby upper-level low to the northwest, combined with breezy east-southeast low-level winds, led to isolated to scattered showers over eastern Puerto Rico early in the morning. As the day progressed, showers and cloudiness spread across much of Puerto Rico, with more frequent and intense showers and isolated thunderstorms developing over the San Juan metropolitan area (particularly over Bayamon, Catano and Toa Baja where flooding and flash flooding reports were received) and parts of north-central to northwestern Puerto Rico during the afternoon. Rainfall totals of up to four inches prompted Flood Advisories and Flash Flood Warnings in the San Juan metro area.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Bayam��n emergency managers reported that Calle 8 (8th Street) in Santa Rosa is flooded due to heavy rains and poor drainage. No vehicles or homes were affected, only the water on the roadway. This road is over a tributary to Rio Hondo (River).

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Debris flow under saturated soils promoted resulted in a fallen tree on Road 139 at KM 18.2 in Sector Carmelita in Ponce. Rainfall accumulations are estimated at approximately 5 inches over the past few days, with an additional 1.30 inches recorded that day (July 18th).
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Debris flow under saturated soils promoted resulted in a fallen tree on Road 139 at KM 18.2 in Sector Carmelita in Ponce.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong afternoon thunderstorm developed over northwestern Puerto Rico on Independence Day. It generated wind gusts of around 40 mph and resulted in a medium to large branch falling from a tree near a parking lot on PR-125 Ave. Emerito Estrada Rivera, at the border of Barrio Pueblo and Barrio Bahomamey, San Sebastian.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A broadcast media meteorologist shared a video of a medium to large branch falling            from a tree near a parking lot on PR-125 (Ave. Emerito Estrada Rivera), at the border of Barrio Pueblo and Barrio Bahomamey, due to a strong thunderstorm that developed ove the area.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A man drowned during the afternoon hours of July 27th after being swept away by rip currents. A moderate risk of rip currents was in effect for the area at that time due to moderate to fresh easterly winds and choppy seas. Additionally, hazy conditions were present.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Broadcast media reported that a man drowned after being swept away by rip currents in Playa Jobos (Jobos Beach) in Isabela.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Major Hurricane Erin moved northeast and north of Puerto Rico and the U.S. Virgin Islands. Erin's Outer Bands and then Feeder Bands still affected the islands, producing strong thunderstorms, gusty winds, and heavy rains across the islands. Several reports of wind gust damage, along with reports of high surf, flooding and debris flow were received between August 16 to 18.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The 911 Call Center reported a fallen utility pole at Camino Concepcion Sanchez (Concepcion Sanchez Way) and blocking the roadway. Electrical cables were also reported as hanging and over the roadway. This incident occurred as the outer bands of Hurricane Erin moved onshore.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A long period northerly swell from the then distant TC Erin and AL90 promoted life-threatening rip currents, rough surf conditions, and Small Craft Advisory conditions during the penultimate weekend of August. Three fatalities and multiple injuries were reported at north-oriented beaches of Puerto Rico.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A 31-year-old male was surfing on August 24th near Kikita Beach Dorado, when he was swept by rip currents. His body was recovered on Tuesday (August 26th) morning.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Major Hurricane Erin moved northeast and north of Puerto Rico and the U.S. Virgin Islands. Erin's Outer Bands and then Feeder Bands still affected the islands, producing strong thunderstorms, gusty winds, and heavy rains across the islands. EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A large, long-period northerly swell moved across the local Atlantic Ocean and Caribbean Passages from late Thursday night on October 2nd through October 6th, creating hazardous marine and coastal conditions across the north-facing beaches of Puerto Rico. The incoming swell generated elevated breaking waves, dangerous rip currents, and episodes of localized coastal erosion. Several north-coast beaches experienced periods of high surf and occasional Coastal Flooding near low-lying areas close to the shore, prompting the issuance of Rip Current Statements, High Surf, and Coastal Flood Advisories. Conditions gradually improved as the swell decayed by October 6th.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Coastal flooding occurred near the Aguadilla Boat Ramp, where Emergency Managers reported that water covered the ramp area around 8:30 PM AST. The inundation limited access to the facility, but no vessels or vehicles were impacted.

A 53-year-old male was exiting his vehicle while holding an umbrella when lightning struck his left arm. The individual sustained injuries and was transported to a regional hospital, where he was reported to be in stable condition. No additional injuries or property damage were reported.
